dashboard.awaitingSignoffHelp: Finished prints waiting for an operator to confirm good/bad before the next job dispatches
dashboard.noActiveProjects: No active projects. Create one on the Projects page and set it Active to track production here.

fleet.title: Fleet
fleet.clickToSelectBatch: Click to select for batch Set Ready
fleet.stoppedConfirmPending: Print stopped from printer screen: confirm outcome below to resume
fleet.stoppedReturnsToService: Print stopped from printer screen, returns to service on next dispatch
fleet.setReadyTitle: Confirm the print was good: credits the part count and returns this printer to the dispatch queue
fleet.badPrintTitle: Mark the print as failed: no parts are credited; the job re-queues so it can print again
fleet.offlineWentOffline: Went offline with a job in progress. If it comes back printing, this clears automatically.
fleet.uploadFailedButComplete: Upload failed, but printer shows job complete. Did the print succeed?
fleet.uploadFailedCheckPrinter: Upload failed after retries, check the printer. Is it actually printing?
fleet.linkJobTitle: Stalled upload? Tell the system which queued job is actually printing on this machine so tracking stays correct
fleet.decommissionMessage: This machine will be removed from the active fleet and will require a manual recommission before running again.
fleet.decommissionSignoffMessage:
Was the last print successful?

This machine will be removed from the active fleet and will require a manual recommission before running again.
fleet.markBadPrintMessage:
This will undo the completed quantity, reopen the part if it was closed, and decommission the printer pending investigation.

Recommission the printer manually once you have confirmed it is safe to run.
fleet.confirmUploadFailureMessage:
This confirms the print never started. No completed quantity will be deducted. The printer will be decommissioned pending investigation.

Recommission the printer manually when it is ready to run again.
fleet.sweepTitle: Manually trigger job dispatch now. This normally happens automatically; use it to start jobs on idle machines without waiting for the next cycle.
fleet.offlineBannerCount_one: {{count}} printer went offline with a job in progress
fleet.offlineBannerCount_other: {{count}} printers went offline with a job in progress
fleet.offlineBannerAutoClear: (will auto-clear if they come back printing)
fleet.uploadBannerCount_one: {{count}} printer had a failed upload, check each machine
fleet.uploadBannerCount_other: {{count}} printers had a failed upload, check each machine
fleet.emptyHint: Your fleet will appear here as live status cards. Add printers one at a time or import your whole farm from a CSV, both are on the Settings page.
fleet.linkJobModalHint: Select the job currently running on this machine.
fleet.linkJobModalEmpty: No failed or stalled jobs found for this printer's model.

printers.title: Printers
printers.subtitle: All printers, grouped by model. Click any row to view history and add notes.
printers.searchPlaceholder: Search by name, model, group, or IP…

printerDetail.notePlaceholder: Describe an observation, inspection result, or any relevant note…
printerDetail.noHistoryYet: No history yet: events are recorded automatically as this printer receives jobs, finishes prints, or changes status.

projects.partStatusOpenHelp: Still needs parts: the scheduler will keep dispatching jobs for it
projects.partStatusCompleteHelp: Target quantity reached: no more jobs will dispatch for this part
projects.partsPerPlateHelp: How many finished parts one plate of this G-code produces: each good print credits this many toward the part's target
projects.filenameTip: Tip: filenames with a model, print time, and weight (e.g. <0>bracket_MK4S_2h30m_45g.gcode</0>) auto-fill these fields; you can adjust them after upload.
projects.bambuRequiresThreemf: Bambu printers require a <0>.3mf</0> file exported from Bambu Studio or Orca Slicer; plain .gcode files are not supported.
projects.errorSelectAmsSlot: Select an AMS slot or External Spool.
projects.targetingHelp: Optional: restrict which printers can run this file. Material/color must match what an operator marked as loaded on the printer; groups restrict dispatch to those printer groups. Leave everything blank to allow any matching printer.
projects.parseFilenameTitle: Re-read print time and material weight from the filename (e.g. …_2h30m_45g.gcode)
projects.noTimeOrMaterialFound: No time or material data found in filename.
projects.closePartMessage: This will close the part and stop dispatching.
projects.reopenPartMessage: This will reopen the part and resume dispatching.
projects.readyToDispatch: Ready to dispatch: a matching idle printer will pick this up on the next sweep.
projects.deleteProjectMessageWithParts: This will permanently delete {{count}} part(s) and all their G-code files. This cannot be undone.
projects.deleteProjectMessageEmpty: This will permanently delete the project. This cannot be undone.
projects.markCompleteMessage: {{count}} open part(s) will be closed and any queued jobs cancelled.
projects.reactivateMessage: Parts with remaining qty will be reopened and dispatch will resume.
projects.allPartsAtTargetWarning: All parts are at target qty, adjust quantities first.
projects.deletePartConfirmMessage: Delete "{{name}}"? This will also delete its G-code files and cannot be undone.
projects.duplicateProjectHint: All parts and G-code files will be copied. The new project starts as a draft with all quantities reset to zero.
projects.title: Projects
projects.emptyHint: Projects are how work flows through the farm: a <0>Project</0> contains <0>Parts</0> (what to print and how many), each part gets <0>G-code</0> uploaded per printer model, and the scheduler dispatches <0>Jobs</0> to idle printers until every part hits its target quantity. Start with “+ New Project” above.
projects.filamentAppliesHint: applies to all gcodes in this project unless overridden per-gcode
projects.noPartsYet: No parts yet. Add one below.

jobs.noJobsMatchFilters: No jobs match the current filters, try clearing them.
jobs.emptyHint: Jobs are created automatically: when a project is Active and one of its parts has G-code uploaded, the scheduler dispatches jobs to idle printers of the matching model. If you expected a job here, check that the project is Active and a matching printer is idle (not held or decommissioned).
jobs.cancelJobMessage: Remove this job from the queue?
jobs.title: Job Queue

decommissioned.recommissionMessage: Only proceed if the machine has been fully inspected and confirmed safe to run. It will return to the active fleet and be eligible to receive jobs immediately.
decommissioned.recommissionPrompt: What was done to fix this printer?
decommissioned.title: Decommissioned
decommissioned.subtitle: Printers removed from the active fleet. Each requires inspection and manual recommission before receiving jobs.
decommissioned.notePlaceholder: Describe the issue, what was inspected, and any findings…
decommissioned.noteHelpText: Enter saves · Shift+Enter newline · Esc cancels

settings.title: Settings
settings.printerModelsHint: Configure which printer models are available in your farm. Models appear in the G-code upload selector and the Add Printer form. Deleting a model is blocked if active printers use it.
settings.noModelsYet: No models configured yet. Add your first model below.
settings.groupsHint: Named groups (e.g. racks or rooms) that projects and G-codes can restrict dispatch to. A group stays listed here even when no printer currently carries it (assigning a printer's Group field on the Printers page auto-registers a new name here too). Deleting a group is blocked while any active printer, G-code, or project still references it.
settings.noGroupsYet: No groups registered yet. Add one below, or it'll be created automatically the first time you type it on a printer.
settings.filamentLibraryHint: Define the filament types and colors available in your farm. Printers and G-codes select from these lists.
settings.noTypesYet: No types yet. Add your first below.
settings.noColorsYet: No colors yet. Add your first below.
settings.addPrinterHint: Add a single printer directly without a CSV file.
settings.noModelsForBrand: No models for this brand yet, add one in Printer Models above first.
settings.importRegistryHint: Upload a CSV with columns: <0>model, name, ip, api_key, group, type</0>.<1></1>Model field value is the ID from the Printer Models list above. Missing mandatory fields and duplicate names are skipped.
settings.farmNameHint: Shown in the sidebar. Name your farm, or leave blank for the default.
settings.dispatchHint: The scheduler keeps this many printers uploading or printing at once, pulling further down the ready queue to fill that target even if some printers have no matching work right now. Reduce this number if your network is saturated during large uploads.
settings.farmBackupHint: Export a full snapshot of your printers, projects, parts, G-code files, and job history. Use the same file to restore on another machine or recover from data loss.
settings.restoreFarmMessage: This will replace ALL current farm data with the backup. This cannot be undone.
settings.pollingHint: All printers are polled every <0>15 seconds</0> via their connector API. Polling runs concurrently: all printers are queried in parallel each tick. Unreachable printers show as <1>{{offline}}</1> and do not affect other printers.
settings.aboutText: Hi, I'm Joel (aka <0>3D Printing Nerd</0>). I built this tool to manage my own print farm and decided to open-source it so the community could benefit too. If it saves you time or headaches, I'd love a coffee, it helps me keep making free content and tools like this one. Thanks for being part of the community. Happy printing!
settings.credentialHelpPrusa: API Key: on the printer under Settings → Network → PrusaLink, or in the PrusaLink web UI (open the printer's IP in a browser) under Settings → API Key.
settings.credentialHelpElegooCentauri: No API key needed, just the printer's IP address, shown on the printer's network settings screen.
settings.credentialHelpElegooCentauri2: Enable LAN mode on the printer. The Access Code and Serial Number are shown on the printer's network settings screen.
settings.credentialHelpBambu: Enable LAN Mode on the printer first. The Access Code is on the printer screen under Settings → WLAN; the Serial Number is under Settings → Device.
settings.credentialHelpKlipper: No API key needed, just the IP of the machine running Moonraker. Port 7125 is used automatically.
settings.credentialHelpOctoprint: API Key: in OctoPrint under Settings → API. If OctoPrint isn't on port 80 (commonly :5000), include the port in the IP field, e.g. 192.168.1.50:5000.

language.title: Language
language.hint: Choose the language used throughout the interface.
